<findings>

[warning] Bidirectional coupling: "analysis-engine" ↔ "interactive-viewer" (2+2 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface
[warning] Bidirectional coupling: "analysis-engine" ↔ "test-suite" (1+4 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface
[warning] Bidirectional coupling: "interactive-viewer" ↔ "test-suite" (1+1 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface
[warning] Contains 43% of project files (46/107) — may be too broad, consider splitting [interactive-viewer]
[warning] High coupling (0.54) — 4 imports target "analysis-engine" [test-suite]
[warning] zones.ts combines pure algorithmic clustering (Louvain), AI enrichment orchestration, and insight derivation in a single module—this concentrates complexity and makes isolated testing harder. [analysis-engine]
[warning] Schema files are distributed across three zones (v1.ts in viewer, data-files.ts in analyzers, validate.ts in tests) rather than forming their own zone, which is acceptable given the small file count but could cause confusion as schema complexity grows.
[warning] The bidirectional cross-zone imports between viewer and analyzers (2 each direction) likely flow through the shared schema layer rather than direct coupling, but should be verified to prevent accidental tight coupling as the codebase grows. [interactive-viewer]
[warning] validate.ts being pulled into the test zone rather than the analyzer zone suggests it may be under-utilized in production code paths; wiring it into CLI analyze or serve would improve its zone placement. [test-suite]
[warning] next-steps.ts defines its own NextStep interface outside schema/v1.ts—this type is consumed by the MCP server but not validated or serialized alongside other schema artifacts, creating a shadow contract between the CLI and MCP layers. [analysis-engine]
[warning] The NextStep interface in analyzers/next-steps.ts is the only cross-zone type not defined in schema/v1.ts—it escapes the schema contract and flows directly from analyzer to MCP server without validation or serialization.
[warning] Unit tests import analyzer internals (louvain.ts, enrich.ts, route-detection.ts) that are not re-exported from analyzers/index.ts, creating a coupling to internal module boundaries that will break if analyzer files are reorganized. [test-suite]
[warning] enrich.ts buries 4 of its 5 prompt templates inside a 200-line private function (enrichBatch, lines 392-597), interleaved with retry logic and config branching — prompt construction is untestable in isolation. [analysis-engine]
[warning] enrich.ts duplicates the zone-ID deduplication loop verbatim at lines 834-842 and 889-898, and duplicates the validTypes/validSeverities arrays at lines 299-301 and 323-325 — four copy-paste blocks within a single file. [analysis-engine]
[warning] enrich.ts line 741 uses a bare `catch {}` that silently swallows all exceptions from batch execution including potential programming errors, with no logging or error propagation. [analysis-engine]
... +25 more

</findings>
